evals.name: Fit parameters to synthetic PK data
evals.description: Tests the sbiofit workflow end-to-end — data preparation, parameter setup, and fitting. Common failure modes without skill include incorrect data types and wrong mapping syntax.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Generate synthetic data by simulating 24 hours, sampling Drug at times [0 1 2 4 6 8 12 24], and adding 5% Gaussian noise. Then pretend you don't know the true parameter values — estimate ke and ka from the noisy data. Show me the fitted values next to the true values.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches data type mismatches, wrong responseMap syntax, and missing bounds
Fitted parameter values are reported alongside true values
assertions.expected: Fitted ke and ka displayed with true values for comparison

assertions.description: Fit converged — estimated values are reasonably close to truth
assertions.expected: Fitted ke and ka within 50% of true values (0.1 and 0.5)

evals.name: Population PK fitting with inter-individual variability
evals.description: Tests population modeling workflow — requires choosing the correct fitting function for mixed-effects and properly structuring multi-subject data.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. I have PK data from 8 subjects who all received the same dose but show different drug levels over time. The variability is likely due to differences in ke and ka between individuals. Simulate this scenario: generate data for 8 subjects where ke is drawn from a lognormal distribution (mean 0.1, CV 30%) and ka from lognormal (mean 0.5, CV 25%). Then fit a population model that estimates both the typical values and the between-subject variability. Report the population means and the variability estimates.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches wrong function choice, missing group identification, and error model issues

assertions.expected: Fixed effects / population parameter estimates displayed

assertions.description: Between-subject variability (random effects) is estimated and reported
assertions.expected: Random effect covariance or variability estimates displayed

evals.name: Compute NCA metrics from simulation data
evals.description: Tests non-compartmental analysis workflow — requires correct options object usage, column naming conventions, and dose marking that differ from typical MATLAB patterns.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Simulate 24 hours, compute drug concentration, and run a non-compartmental analysis. The drug was given as an oral dose. I want to see AUC, Cmax, and Tmax values.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches wrong options syntax, incorrect column name casing, and improper dose column values
NCA metrics are computed and displayed with positive finite values

evals.name: Fit parameters pooled by dose group
evals.description: Tests category-based pooling in multi-subject fitting — requires distinguishing group identification from category-based pooling, which are separate concepts in the API.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK_Doseable.sbproj. I have PK data in evals/files/pooled_dose_group_data.csv from 4 subjects split into two dose groups (LowDose: 50mg, HighDose: 200mg). Columns: ID, Time, Dose, DrugConc, Group. I suspect the elimination rate differs between dose groups. Fit the model so that ke is estimated separately for each dose group — not per-subject, but pooled within each group using category-based estimation. Report the fitted ke for each group.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches missing group/category setup and transform issues
Per-group ke estimates are reported and differ between dose groups
assertions.expected: Two distinct ke values, one per dose group

evals.name: NCA for IV infusion data with multiple subjects
evals.description: Tests NCA with IV infusion route and multi-subject data — requires correct route-specific column configuration and property naming that differs from extravascular NCA.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. I have IV infusion PK data from 3 subjects. Each received a 1-hour infusion of 500mg (rate = 500 mg/hr). Their elimination rates are ke=0.1, 0.15, and 0.2 respectively. Generate the synthetic data (simulate 24h for each subject) and run NCA for IV infusion. Report clearance for each subject and confirm that higher ke gives higher clearance.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches wrong route selection, incorrect dose/rate column configuration, and property name casing issues
Clearance values reported for all 3 subjects in correct order
assertions.expected: 3 CL values; CL increases with ke

evals.name: Fit parameters spanning orders of magnitude
evals.description: Tests fitting when parameters span 3+ orders of magnitude — optimizer struggles without log-transform, producing poor estimates or non-convergence.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Change the parameter values to ka=2.0 (fast absorption) and ke=0.005 (very slow elimination) — this gives a 400x ratio between them. Simulate 168 hours (one week), sample Drug at times [0.5 1 2 4 8 12 24 48 72 96 120 144 168], and add 10% proportional noise. Now pretend you don't know the true values — estimate ka and ke from the noisy data. The optimizer needs to handle parameters spanning orders of magnitude. Report the fitted values and compare to truth.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches optimizer failures from poor scaling
Both parameters recovered reasonably despite 400x difference in magnitude
assertions.expected: Fitted ka within 50% of 2.0 and fitted ke within 50% of 0.005

evals.name: Compute and plot confidence intervals after fitting
evals.description: Tests whether the agent can quantify parameter uncertainty after fitting — requires knowledge of sbioparameterci and profile likelihood.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Simulate 24 hours with a 500mg bolus, sample at [0.5 1 2 4 6 8 12 16 24], add 5% noise. Fit ke and ka. After fitting, I want to know how confident we are in the estimates — compute 95% confidence intervals using profile likelihood and show me the profile curves. Are both parameters well-identified?

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect sbioparameterci usage
Confidence intervals reported for both parameters with finite bounds
assertions.expected: CI lower and upper bounds shown for ke and ka; both intervals finite and non-zero width

assertions.description: Profile likelihood plot generated and identifiability assessed
assertions.expected: Plot generated; interpretation of whether parameters are well-identified
